title: Is the zero-divisor graph Γ(Z_101 × Z_25) prime?
generalization_signal: At p = 101 the four-class Γ(Z_p × Z_25) line still closes in the campaign's high-barrier complementary-support language: A is the fixed A0 reservoir, B = {1,263,269,271} is a zero-spill barrier set above half the interval, and C can be chosen as a size-100 support-avoiding block.
strongest_honest_claim: An explicit prime-labeling witness for Γ(Z_101 × Z_25) survives skeptical verification. The instance is strong feeder evidence that the active high-barrier complementary-support package still works at the first reopened post-97 boundary, but this artifact by itself is still only instance-level evidence rather than a proved new theorem slice.
candidate_theorem_slice: A wrapper-language complementary-support corollary for Γ(Z_p × Z_25): if the interval {1, ..., 5p + 19} contains a size p - 1 block C whose prime support avoids {2,3,b1,b2,b3} for distinct barrier primes b1,b2,b3 above half the interval, then with A = A0, B = {1,b1,b2,b3}, and D the complement, the full graph Γ(Z_p × Z_25) is prime.
next_action: Promote the verified p = 101 feeder into the family dossier, then test z103-z25-prime-zero-divisor-graph while prioritizing a wrapper-language complementary-support C-supply corollary over exact-instance Lean.
